what_it_does: Implements a variable, data-driven projection engine that maps event records into polar coordinates (theta, r) and dyadic grid cells according to a binding (lens). It resolves angular sectors from the binding's declared source, computes radius from time, address-tree distance, semantic cosine distance, a two-pole separator, or a type-nucleation fit, and emits points, sector labels, typed edges, and diagnostic reports for rendering.
observations: The file is heavily commented with design rationale: no hardcoded sectors, operator-law humanization, pure-read floor, and anti-green-paint honesty checks.
It defensively imports several legibility registries (kinds, node-types, groups, projection spaces, cascade flow) and falls back to empty dicts rather than failing.
BindingRegistry discovers lens definitions from a bindings directory via importlib and validates required fields.
project() supports multiple radius modes: time-from-now, structural address distance, semantic cosine distance, two-gravity separator, and nucleation type-fit.
Semantic mode min-max normalizes cosine distances and computes strain as the gap between structural filing distance and semantic meaning distance.
Separator mode runs a separation_report (the fifth gate) on raw cosines to decide whether two poles actually separate the corpus.
Nucleation mode runs agglomerative clustering, a permutation null test for distinctness, and a 20/80 birth threshold to surface candidate new types and dissolution candidates.
_human_summary strips machine-log prefixes and keeps only genuine human tails for chat, annotation, and trial.turn kinds, with address guarding.
Sectors can be resolved from distinct kinds, declared kind-groups, registry rows, or graph nodes, with optional topological ordering by edges.
A stable hash-based jitter determines each point's angle within its sector, and a dyadic grid cell is derived from the event address path.
standouts: separation_report: a multi-check discriminator (pole distinctness, per-pole spread, Spearman rank correlation, balance) computed on raw cosines before any cosmetic normalization.
nucleation_report: type-birth analysis using agglomerative clustering, a pile-permutation null for distinctness, and surfaced dissolution candidates.
_human_summary: a boundary-layer humanization function that enforces the operator-law by translating machine event summaries into operator-facing text.
project(): a single large function that orchestrates five different radius semantics plus sector resolution, edge surfacing, and metadata enrichment.
conventions: Defensive imports with empty-dict fallbacks to keep projection from breaking when a registry is missing.
Fail-loud ValueError when a specialized radius mode lacks required input vectors, poles, or nucleation report.
Registry-is-truth: human names and meanings are read from declared meta registries; unseeded ids fall back to a humanized title-case string.
Pure-read parameter passing: vectors, poles, and nucleation reports are supplied by the caller; the module does not fetch store data itself.
Event-to-row edge uses a singularization rule on registry names (e.g., roles -> role).
Deterministic stable hashing for pseudo-random angular placement within a sector.
Lazy import of runtime.scale inside nucleation_report to avoid a hard dependency until needed.
concerns: project() is a long, multi-mode monolith with high cyclomatic complexity, making it hard to test and modify in isolation.
_sector_index's kind-group fallback returns the last declared sector for unmapped kinds, which could silently dump unrelated events into a remainder sector.
In semantic/separator modes, vectorless points are placed at the rim and flagged but still rendered, which may crowd the outer edge.
_human_summary's provenance-address regex may over-strip legitimate human text that happens to contain scheme-like tokens.
BindingRegistry.discover executes arbitrary Python files from the bindings directory, which is a code-execution surface.
_resolve_sectors behaves differently for whole_set graph bindings (drops unmapped events) versus non-whole_set (adds a '—' remainder), which could surprise consumers.
notes: A dense, philosophy-heavy pure-read geometry engine that turns event streams into a parameterized polar visualization through multiple swappable lenses, with explicit honesty checks to avoid cosmetic green-paint.
questions: Where are the binding file schemas and meta fields documented?
How does the bridge/store key vectors by address, and which events carry source_address versus address?
What is the exact format of sector_edges and how are relation directions produced elsewhere?
How are per-type admission radii (type_radii) computed before nucleation_report is called?
Is there a test suite covering all radius modes and the separation/nucleation diagnostics?
What frontend contract consumes points, strain segments, and directed sector edges?
apparent_themes: Pure read and fail-loud over silent fallback
Honest visualization that surfaces normalization and degeneracy
intention_signals: To build a general 'lens' instrument where any angular or radial source can be swapped in, while keeping the rendering honest about real structure versus cosmetic normalization.
novelty: The combination of a universal projection equation with explicit anti-degeneracy gates (separation_report, nucleation permutation null) and an operator-law humanization layer at the data boundary.
contribution: A reusable, binding-driven event-to-geometry projector with built-in diagnostics for semantic, two-pole, and type-nucleation views, plus registry-based human-readable metadata resolution.
summary_for_embedding: company/runtime/projection.py is a data-driven polar projection engine that maps timestamped events into sectors and radii according to swappable bindings, supporting time, address, semantic, two-pole separator, and type-nucleation radius modes with honesty checks and human-readable labels.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a variable, pure-read geometry layer that renders event data through multiple lenses without hardcoded sectors or silent fallbacks.

symbols.does: Fallback human label generator for unseeded machine ids.
symbols.description: Splits a machine id on separators, title-cases the words, and returns a readable string so operators never see raw ids.

symbols.does: Resolves a human name for a kind or sector id from a meta registry.
symbols.description: Looks up meta.name first, then falls back to _humanize_kind, keeping displayed labels declared-first and legible.

symbols.does: Resolves a one-line meaning string for a kind or sector id.
symbols.description: Returns meta.is if present, otherwise None, so the UI can show a short description without fabricating one.

symbols.does: Produces an operator-facing summary from an event's machine summary.
symbols.description: For chat, annotation, and trial.turn it extracts the human tail and address-guards it; for all other kinds it returns empty because meaning lives in kind_meaning.

symbols.does: Cleans a kept human tail by removing machine addresses and tidying whitespace.
symbols.description: Submits the retained text to operator-law by stripping provenance tokens, collapsing whitespace, and trimming dangling separators.

symbols.does: Discovers and holds lens bindings from the bindings directory.
symbols.description: A registry class that loads binding modules from disk, validates their BINDING dicts, and provides lookup/list access.

symbols.does: Initializes an empty binding row store.
symbols.description: Creates the internal _rows dict used to hold discovered bindings.

symbols.does: Loads all bindings/*.py files and validates them.
symbols.description: Uses importlib to execute binding modules, checks required fields and id consistency, and raises ValueError on malformed bindings.

symbols.does: Retrieves a binding by id or a sensible default.
symbols.description: Returns the requested binding if present, otherwise falls back to 'raw' or the alphabetically first binding, synthesizing a raw-kinds binding if none exist.

symbols.does: Returns all discovered bindings sorted by id.
symbols.description: Provides a sorted list of binding dicts for UI lens selection.

symbols.does: Maps a string to a deterministic float in [0,1).
symbols.description: Uses the first eight hex digits of SHA-256 to produce a reproducible pseudo-random value for angular jitter.

symbols.does: Parses an ISO timestamp string into a datetime.
symbols.description: Wraps datetime.fromisoformat and returns None on failure.

symbols.does: Parses the ?at= scrubber value into an aware datetime.
symbols.description: Accepts epoch seconds or ISO strings and returns UTC datetime, or None for real now.

symbols.does: Extracts the primary address from an event.
symbols.description: Prefers 'address', falls back to 'source_address', and returns an empty string if neither is present.

symbols.does: Computes tree distance between two hierarchical addresses.
symbols.description: Strips scheme prefixes, splits on '/', measures common-prefix length, and returns the sum of remaining segment counts.

symbols.does: Normalizes an address into path segments.
symbols.description: Removes the ui:// scheme if present and filters empty segments so tree distance is scheme-agnostic.

symbols.does: Computes cosine similarity between two vectors.
symbols.description: Mirrors an external implementation, fails loudly on dimension mismatch or zero vectors, and avoids silent fabrication.

symbols.does: Computes Spearman rank correlation between two lists.
symbols.description: Ranks values with average-rank ties and returns Pearson on ranks; returns 0.0 for insufficient or zero-variance data.

symbols.does: Assigns average 1-based ranks with tie handling.
symbols.description: Sorts indices, detects equal-value blocks, and assigns the mean rank across each tied block.

symbols.does: Diagnoses whether two poles actually separate a corpus.
symbols.description: The fifth gate: checks raw pole distinctness, per-pole spread, Spearman redundancy, and balance to decide if a two-gravity field is real.

symbols.does: Computes population standard deviation of raw cosines.
symbols.description: Used to measure whether each pole's pull varies across the corpus.

symbols.does: Formats leader entries for the separation report.
symbols.description: Builds lean/pull metadata for the items most strongly attracted to each pole.

symbols.does: Unit-normalizes a vector.
symbols.description: Divides each component by the Euclidean magnitude, guarding against division by zero.

symbols.does: Computes the unit-normalized mean of a set of vectors.
symbols.description: Sums component-wise and normalizes to obtain the group's central direction.

symbols.does: Analyzes items against registered types to find under-coverage and candidate new types.
symbols.description: Implements the 20/80 water-law: fits items to types by their own admission radii, clusters the outside pile, tests distinctness with a permutation null, and surfaces birth/dissolution candidates.

symbols.does: Computes silhouette-like margin for a candidate cluster.
symbols.description: Measures mean internal cohesion minus mean nearest-existing-type similarity to judge whether a pile is its own thing.

symbols.does: Converts a plural registry name to its singular event field.
symbols.description: Applies simple rules (ies->y, trailing s dropped) so events can be mapped to registry rows generically.

symbols.does: Determines which row of an entity-set an event relates to.
symbols.description: For graphs returns the node/from_node; for registries uses the singularized field; returns None when no row is named.

symbols.does: Topologically sorts sector ids using directed edges.
symbols.description: Kahn's algorithm with a stable deterministic tie-break; cycles are appended rather than dropped.

symbols.does: Resolves the ordered list of sectors from a binding's angle_from.
symbols.description: Handles kind, kind-group, registry rows, and graph nodes, with count or edge-based ordering and an honest remainder sector when appropriate.

symbols.does: Finds the sector index for a kind under the current kmap.
symbols.description: Supports fnmatch group matching and falls back to the last declared group for unmapped kinds.

symbols.does: Computes a dyadic grid cell from an address path.
symbols.description: Hashes each path segment into one of four quadrants and builds an (i,j,d) cell MSB-first so parent cells contain children.

symbols.does: Main entry point that projects events into points and geometry metadata.
symbols.description: Parses timestamps, resolves sectors, precomputes semantic/separator/nucleation values, builds per-point records with human labels, and returns sectors, edges, rings, and diagnostic reports.

symbols.does: Checks whether a stamped item is the semantic centre.
symbols.description: Compares source_address or address against the address centre so the centre can be excluded from normalization bands.
